question.text: What is the main function of the Financial Intelligence Unit - India (FIU-IND)?

answer.text: Receiving, processing, analysing and disseminating information relating to suspect financial transactions
Investigating and prosecuting financial crimes directly
Regulating banking sector compliance with anti-money laundering norms
Managing the national database of all financial transactions in India

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FIU-IND was set up in November 2004 as the central national agency responsible for receiving, processing, analysing and disseminating information relating to suspect financial transactions. It coordinates efforts against money laundering and financing of terrorism. **The Trap:** FIU-IND does not investigate or prosecute crimes directly — it analyses and disseminates intelligence. It is not a regulator; it is an intelligence body reporting to the Economic Intelligence Council. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** FIU-IND receives CTRs, STRs, CBWTRs, NTRs, and IPRs from reporting entities. Know these report types for the exam.
